document.docAuthor: Wang, Xiao-Ya, Zhi, Yan & Chen, Xiang-Sheng
document.masterDocTitle: Five new species of the genus Andes Stål, 1866 from China (Hemiptera, Fulgoromorpha, Cixiidae)

mods:titleInfo.mods:title: Five new species of the genus Andes Stål, 1866 from China (Hemiptera, Fulgoromorpha, Cixiidae)

mods:name.mods:affiliation: Institute of Entomology, Guizhou University, Guiyang, Guizhou 550025, P. R. China. & The Provincial Special Key Laboratory for Development and Utilization of Insect Resources, Guizhou University, Guiyang, Guizhou 550025, P. R. China.
Laboratory Animal Center, Guizhou Medical University, Guiyang, Guizhou 550025, P. R. China.

Institute of Entomology, Guizhou University, Guiyang, Guizhou 550025, P. R. China. & The Provincial Special Key Laboratory for Development and Utilization of Insect Resources, Guizhou University, Guiyang, Guizhou 550025, P. R. China.

paragraph.treatmentCitationGroup:
Andes
–
Muir 1925: 201
. —
Zhou et al. 1985: 23
. —
Tsaur et al. 1991: 67
.

subSubSection.paragraph:
Andes undulatus Stål, 1870
(subsequent designation in Muir 1925 ).

Vertex narrowest and transverse at apex, widening to base, with U- or V-shaped basal emargination; lateral carinae strongly elevated; median carina absent. Forewing in resting position steeply tectiform; ScP +RA, RP and MP arising separately from a common point on basal cell or forming a minute common stem. Fore coxa with outer margin straight, subparallel with inner margin, not produced. Hind tibiae without or with several very small lateral spines. Chaetotaxy of hindtarsi 7–9/7–8.
Palaearctic Region , Oriental Region , Australian Region and Afrotropical Region .

paragraph.emphasis:
Checklist and distribution of Chinese species of
Andes Stål, 1866

subSubSection.paragraph:
A. balteiformis Wang, Zhi & Chen
sp. nov. ; China ( Guizhou , Guangxi , Fujian ).

A. bifidus Wang, Zhi & Chen
sp. nov. ; China ( Yunnan ).

A. furcutus Wang, Zhang & Chen
sp. nov. ; China ( Guizhou ).

A. latanalus Wang & Chen
sp. nov. ; China ( Guangxi ).

A. luzonensis Tsaur & Hsu, 1991
; China ( Taiwan) .

A. marmoratus ( Uhler, 1896 )
; China ( Beijing ), Japan ( Hokkaido , Honshu, Kyushu and Shikoku).
A. noctua Fennah, 1956
; China ( Hubei ).

A. notatus Tsaur & Hsu, 1991
; China ( Taiwan) .

A. pallidus Wang & Chen
sp. nov. ; China ( Guizhou ).

paragraph.emphasis:
Key to males of Chinese species of
Andes Stål, 1866

paragraph.keyLead: 2. Frons and lateral margin of vertex with alternating transverse stripes.............................................3
– Frons and lateral margin of vertex without alternating transverse stripes.......................................5

3. Tegmen with clavus smoky brown at base; tegminal apex near apical angle distinctly dark brown ( Ishihara 1957 : fig. 1t ) ...........................................................................
A. marmoratus ( Uhler, 1896 )

4. Medioventral process of pygofer triangular; periandrium with a long, strongly sinuate spine, directed cephalad ( Fennah 1956 : fig. 1h) ......................................................
A. noctua Fennah, 1956

– Medioventral process of pygofer semicircular; periandrium with a long, slender, C-shaped spine, directed ventrad; female with reduced wax secreting plates (
Tsaur et al. 1991
: fig. 34g –i, l) ....... .....................................................................................................
A. formosanus ( Matsumura, 1914 )

5. Periandrium with a long, slender, S-shaped spine with its tip directed dorsad (
Tsaur et al. 1991
: fig. 35h–i) .............................................................................................
A. notatus Tsaur & Hsu, 1991

7. Apical margin of gonostyli deeply emarginate, forming two processes ( Fig. 3G, I ) .................... ...........................................................................................
A. bifidus Wang, Zhi & Chen
sp. nov.

8. Periandrium on left laterally with a sclerotized, belt-shaped process arising ventrally near apex ( Fig. 1J–M ) .....................................................
A. balteiformis Wang, Zhi & Chen
sp. nov.

9. Anal segment wider than long in dorsal view ( Fig. 7H ) ....
A. latanalus Wang & Chen
sp. nov.

10. Ventral margin of periandrium apically with a bifurcated spinose process ( Fig. 9J–M ) .............. .....................................................................................................
A. pallidus Wang & Chen
sp. nov.

11. Dorsal margin of periandrium with a short laminal process at about midlength, basal part wide and apical margin bifurcated ( Fig. 5J–M ) .........
A. furcutus Wang, Zhang & Chen
sp. nov.

– Endosoma of aedeagus basally without spinose processes; endosoma coarsely shagreen or subfimbriate ( Fennah 1956 : fig. 2m ) .............................................................
A. lachesis Fennah, 1956
